what: A license can be present yet not open for reuse
title: Beyond F-UJI: reuse, sensitivity, hygiene, and FAIR-TLC
text: Detecting license exists says nothing whether may reuse data. license_reuse() classifies actual permissions, maps license six-category taxonomy (Re)usable Data Project (Carbon et al. 2019). permissive licenses facilitate reuse without negotiation; CC--NC-ND present standard, yet restrictive.
code: license_reuse("https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/")[c("category", "rdp_category", "facilitates_reuse")] #> $category #> [1] "open (attribution)" #>  #> $rdp_category #> [1] "permissive" #>  #> $facilitates_reuse #> [1] TRUE license_reuse("https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/")[c("category", "rdp_category", "facilitates_reuse")] #> $category #> [1] "restrictive (non-commercial, no-derivatives)" #>  #> $rdp_category #> [1] "restrictive" #>  #> $facilitates_reuse #> [1] FALSE

what: Controlled-access and sensitive data is not a FAIR failure
title: Beyond F-UJI: reuse, sensitivity, hygiene, and FAIR-TLC
text: Data behind data-use agreement (e.g. human/clinical data) legitimately restricted; judged metadata richness, open download. classify_access() flags , drawing (Re)usable Data Project curations.
code: classify_access(access_level = "closedAccess",                 urls = "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gap/?term=phs000424")[c("access", "controlled_access", "sensitive")] #> $access #> [1] "closed" #>  #> $controlled_access #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $sensitive #> [1] TRUE

title: Beyond F-UJI: reuse, sensitivity, hygiene, and FAIR-TLC
text: Layered identifiers (identifier minted top another) non-persistent identifiers reduce interoperability.
code: identifier_hygiene("RRID:MGI:5577054")$issues #> [1] "Compound/layered identifier: an identifier minted on top of another (e.g. RRID:MGI:...) reduces interoperability; prefer the underlying source PID." #> [2] "Identifier scheme not recognized; may not follow identifier best practices." identifier_hygiene("https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.8347772")$hygiene_ok #> [1] TRUE

title: Beyond F-UJI: reuse, sensitivity, hygiene, and FAIR-TLC
text: reviewer’s framework extends FAIR three principles (Haendel et al., FAIR+): data Traceable (provenance, attribution), Licensed (clearly reusably), Connected (qualified links related entities). fair_tlc() computes assessment.
code: a <- assess_fair("https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.8347772") fair_tlc(a) #>   dimension                             indicator   met #> 1 Traceable                         T1 Provenance  TRUE #> 2 Traceable                        T2 Attribution  TRUE #> 3  Licensed L1 Documented & minimally restrictive  TRUE #> 4  Licensed           L2 Flowthrough transparency  TRUE #> 5 Connected                      C1 Connectedness  TRUE

what: 1. Background: FAIR, the FAIRsFAIR metrics, and F-UJI
title: How rfair works: methodology and architecture
text: FAIR principles (Wilkinson et al. 2016) state research data Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable. aspirational; assess real data object need measurable indicators. FAIRsFAIR project turned principles concrete, testable metric set, F-UJI tool (Devaraju & Huber, PANGAEA) implemented automated assessment service . F-UJI Python web service: send persistent identifier (PID) returns per-metric scores. rfair native R reimplementation F-UJI metrics (version 0.8). performs whole assessment R, external server, assessments scriptable, reproducible, embeddable R pipelines. original rfair package (v1) HTTP client F-UJI server; version (v2) engine .

title: How rfair works: methodology and architecture
text: single call assess_fair() runs pipeline:
code: identifier    │  id_parse()            scheme detection + normalization + resolver URL    ▼ resolution                  content-negotiated GET, follow redirects -> landing page    │  resolve_landing_page()    ▼ harvesting                  a sequence of collectors, in priority order:    │   collect_html_meta()      embedded JSON-LD (schema.org), Dublin Core,    │                            OpenGraph, Highwire meta tags    │   collect_signposting()    HTTP Link header + <link rel> typed links    │   collect_datacite()       DataCite JSON via content negotiation    │   collect_xml()            DataCite XML, Dublin Core, MODS, EML, ISO19139    │   collect_rdf()            JSON-LD (native) and Turtle/RDF-XML (via rdflib)    │   collect_github()         GitHub repository + codemeta.json + CITATION.cff    │   harvest_data()           HEAD on data links for MIME type and size    ▼ mapping + merging           each source is mapped to one reference schema and    │  merge_metadata()         merged (first-non-empty for scalars; union for    │                           lists; longer-but-similar replacement)    ▼ evaluation                  one evaluator per metric inspects the merged metadata    │  run_evaluators()         and the resolved identifier, scoring each test    ▼ scoring                     per-test scores -> per-metric -> F/A/I/R -> overall    │  get_assessment_summary()    ▼ fair_assessment             tidy S3 object (print / summary / as.data.frame /                             as_fair_json / as_rdf)

title: How rfair works: methodology and architecture
text: id_parse() recognizes DOIs, Handles, ARKs, URNs, UUIDs, identifiers.org PIDs, w3id, plain URLs, normalizes , constructs resolver URL. Persistence inferred scheme.
code: id_parse("https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.8347772")[c("preferred_schema", "is_persistent", "identifier_url")] #> $preferred_schema #> [1] "doi" #>  #> $is_persistent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $identifier_url #> [1] "https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.8347772"

title: How rfair works: methodology and architecture
text: Different repositories expose metadata different ways. rfair asks several representations object via HTTP content negotiation (Accept header) scrapes landing page, merges everything single reference schema (~30 elements: creator, title, publisher, publication_date, license, access_level, object_content_identifier, related_resources, …). two sources disagree, scalars keep first non-empty value (replaced longer, sufficiently-similar string), list-valued elements unioned.

title: How rfair works: methodology and architecture
text: Metrics data-driven: definitions, tests, scores, maturity levels come bundled FAIRsFAIR YAML, hard-coded R logic. metric one tests. test contributes score maturity level (CMMI level 0–3: incomplete, initial, moderate, advanced) passes. Metrics use one two scoring mechanisms: cumulative — passed tests’ scores add ; alternative — tests alternative routes points (earned score capped metric total). criterium engine (criterium_engine.R) builds metric’s result YAML lets evaluators mark tests passed; as_fair_json() emits payload matching upstream F-UJI FAIRResults schema.

title: Getting started with rfair
text: Pass DOI, persistent identifier, URL assess_fair(). resolves identifier, harvests metadata, scores FAIRsFAIR metrics. returned fair_assessment object prints F///R summary. numbers come 17 metrics; one row : summary() gives per-principle score table, maturity column reports 0–3 CMMI level (incomplete → advanced).
code: a <- assess_fair("https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.8347772") a as.data.frame(a) summary(a)

title: Getting started with rfair
text: Automated FAIR scores well-known blind spots. rfair surfaces three: can call directly :
code: # A license being *present* does not mean the data is open for reuse. a$reuse                    # per-license: open / restrictive, commercial, derivatives  # Restricted access can be legitimate (e.g. sensitive human data) and should not # be read as "not FAIR". a$access                   # access level, controlled_access, sensitive  # Identifiers should follow best practices. a$identifier_hygiene       # layered / non-persistent identifier warnings license_reuse("https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/") identifier_hygiene("RRID:MGI:5577054") fair_principles()          # canonical FAIR principle definitions

what: Assess the FAIRness of Research Data Objects and Software
title: Assess the FAIRness of Research Data Objects and Software
text: Assess FAIRness research data objects, natively R. rfair native R implementation F-UJI (FAIRsFAIR Research Data Object Assessment) metrics. Given persistent identifier URL resolves object, harvests metadata landing page registries (DataCite, Crossref, GitHub), scores FAIRsFAIR metrics (v0.8 default) Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, Reusability. Unlike original rfair (HTTP client external F-UJI server), version performs entire assessment R — Python, server. also goes beyond F-UJI checks automated FAIR tools usually miss (prompted peer review COVID-19 FAIR-assessment study): whether license actually permits reuse, whether data controlled-access / sensitive, whether identifiers follow best practices.

title: Assess the FAIRness of Research Data Objects and Software

title: Assess the FAIRness of Research Data Objects and Software
text: rfair bundles current F-UJI website choices plus release-specific legacy metric files upstream F-UJI: Supported metadata service type labels include OAI-PMH, OGC CSW, SPARQL, DCAT, schema.org JSON-LD, DataCite, Crossref, Signposting, typed links, RO-Crate, CKAN, generic metadata document option.
code: rfair_metric_versions() #> 0.8 0.5 0.5ssv2 0.5ss 0.5env 0.7_software ...  assess_fair(   "https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.8347772",   metric_version = "0.5ssv2",   use_datacite = TRUE,   metadata_service_endpoint = "https://example.org/oai",   metadata_service_type = "oai_pmh" )

title: Assess the FAIRness of Research Data Objects and Software
text: results attached every assessment ($reuse, $access, $identifier_hygiene) shown app.
code: # A license can be present yet NOT open for reuse license_reuse("https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/")$is_open #> [1] FALSE  # Controlled-access / sensitive data is not a FAIR failure classify_access(access_level = "closedAccess",                 urls = "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gap/...")$controlled_access #> [1] TRUE  # Identifier hygiene (layered / non-persistent PIDs) identifier_hygiene("RRID:MGI:5577054")$issues  # Canonical FAIR principle definitions (go-fair.org / FAIR-nanopubs) fair_principles("R")

title: Assess the FAIRness of Research Data Objects and Software
text: assess_fair_batch() scores vector identifiers returns one tidy row per identifier. assess_data_code() bridges rtransparent: takes data code identifiers rtransparent extracts articles (open_data_links open_code_links columns; DOIs, repository URLs, identifiers.org prefix:accession codes geo:GSE…) scores , using FsF data metrics data FRSM software metrics code. split_identifiers() parses " ; "-joined link strings .
code: rt <- rtransparent::rt_data_code_pmc(xml)          # is_open_data, open_data_links, ... scores <- assess_data_code(rt, id_col = "pmid")    # one row per (article, data/code link)

what: Assess the FAIRness of the data and code shared in articles (rtransparent) — assess_data_code
title: Assess the FAIRness of the data and code shared in articles (rtransparent) — assess_data_code
text: Bridges rtransparent rfair: takes data/code identifiers rtransparent extracts articles (open_data_links open_code_links columns) scores FAIR metrics. Data identifiers scored FsF data metrics code repositories FRSM software metrics.

title: Assess the FAIRness of the data and code shared in articles (rtransparent) — assess_data_code
code: assess_data_code(   x,   id_col = NULL,   data_metric_version = "0.8",   code_metric_version = "0.7_software",   data_col = "open_data_links",   code_col = "open_code_links",   sep = " ; ",   quiet = FALSE,   ... )

title: Assess the FAIRness of the data and code shared in articles (rtransparent) — assess_data_code
text: x One : data frame rtransparent::rt_data_code_pmc() / rt_all_pmc() (open_data_links / open_code_links columns); named list elements; character vector " ; "-joined data-link strings. id_col Optional name column x identifying source article (e.g. "pmid" "doi"); used label result. data_metric_version Metric version data identifiers (default "0.8"). code_metric_version Metric version code repositories (default "0.7_software"). data_col, code_col Column/element names holding joined links (defaults match rtransparent: "open_data_links", "open_code_links"). sep Separator rtransparent uses join identifiers (default " ; "). quiet FALSE (default), print per-identifier progress. ... Passed assess_fair().

what: Assess the FAIRness of a research data object. — assess_fair
title: Assess the FAIRness of a research data object. — assess_fair
text: Resolves persistent identifier URL, harvests metadata, scores FAIRsFAIR metrics, entirely R.

title: Assess the FAIRness of a research data object. — assess_fair
code: assess_fair(   id,   metric_version = "0.8",   use_datacite = TRUE,   metadata_service_endpoint = NULL,   metadata_service_type = metadata_service_types(),   test_debug = FALSE,   resolve = TRUE,   timeout = 15,   use_headless = FALSE )

title: Assess the FAIRness of a research data object. — assess_fair
text: id persistent identifier URL (DOI, Handle, ARK, URN, ...). metric_version Metric version use (see rfair_metric_versions()). use_datacite Whether query DataCite registry metadata. metadata_service_endpoint Optional URL additional metadata document harvest, ready protocol query URL (example OAI-PMH GetRecord URL, OGC CSW GetRecordById URL, SPARQL query URL, DCAT / schema.org JSON-LD / RO-Crate / DataCite / Crossref / CKAN document). response parsed format-gated collectors used content negotiation, recognized metadata document contributes. metadata_service_type Type hint metadata_service_endpoint. "schema_org" harvested JSON-LD; others tried XML metadata document, RDF. test_debug TRUE, collect debug log messages result. resolve TRUE, resolve identifier landing page. timeout Per-request timeout seconds. use_headless TRUE optional chromote package installed, render JavaScript-heavy landing pages headless browser harvesting embedded metadata.

title: Assess the FAIRness of a research data object. — assess_fair
text: fair_assessment object.

title: Assess the FAIRness of a research data object. — assess_fair

title: FAIR-TLC indicators (Traceable, Licensed, Connected) — fair_tlc

title: Plot a FAIR assessment as a scorecard — plot.fair_assessment

code: # S3 method for class 'fair_assessment' plot(   x,   type = c("category", "metric", "sunburst"),   colors = .fair_cat_colors,   show_maturity = (match.arg(type) == "category"),   main = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot a FAIR assessment as a scorecard — plot.fair_assessment
text: x fair_assessment object returned assess_fair(). type draw. "category" (default) draws one bar per FAIR category (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) plus overall score; "metric" draws one bar per individual metric, grouped colored category; "sunburst" draws concentric sunburst (inner ring F///R categories outer ring individual metrics, filled proportion score) overall FAIR percentage center. colors Named character vector category fill colors, names "F", "", "", "R". show_maturity Logical; annotate bar maturity level. Defaults TRUE type = "category". main Title. Defaults resolved identifier (input id). ... Ignored (S3 method compatibility).
